title: Astraea gardneri
intro: Astraea gardneri, also known as the starflower or blue star, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Astraea gardneri include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as other compounds like saponins and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Astraea gardneri is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment for skin conditions, and it is also used in tinctures and capsules to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Astraea gardneri is a species within the family Ostropaceae. It belongs to the order Ostropales and class Dothideomycetes. This species is classified as a lichenized fungus, a combination of a fungus and a photosynthetic partner, typically an alga or cyanobacterium. The family Ostropaceae is a group of lichen-forming fungi. Further classification of Astraea gardneri includes it being a member of the genus Astraea.
